info.title: Anaplan OAuth 2.0 Service API
info.description: Anaplan's OAuth 2.0 service supports two authorization flows: Authorization Code Grant (supports SSO/SAML and basic authentication) and Device Authorization Grant (basic authentication only). Use these endpoints to obtain access tokens, ID tokens, and refresh tokens for Anaplan APIs.

servers.description: US1 (Data Center - US East), US2 (Data Center - US West), US5 (Cloud - US East), US7 (Cloud - US), AP1 (Cloud - Japan), EU1 (Data Center - Netherlands), EU2 (Data Center - Germany), EU4 (Cloud - Europe)

get.description:
Minimum role: None.

Redirects the user's browser to the Anaplan login page to begin the Authorization Code flow. On successful authentication, the browser is redirected to `redirect_uri` with an authorization `code` query parameter. Exchange that code for tokens via `POST /oauth/token`.

**SSO:** If the user's workspace enforces SAML SSO, this endpoint initiates the SSO login. SSO is not available via the Device Authorization Grant flow.

**Note:** This endpoint does not accept an Authorization header. Authentication occurs interactively via the browser redirect.

**Implementation note:** The `anaplan-sdk` library uses `GET /auth/prelogin` (same query parameters) as its `authorization_url` instead of this endpoint. `/auth/prelogin` returns the login page HTML directly (200) whereas `/auth/authorize` returns a 302 redirect to it. Both paths are valid entry points for the Authorization Code flow.

parameters.name: response_type
parameters.description: Must be `code` to initiate the Authorization Code flow.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: Your application's OAuth client ID, found in Anaplan Administration > OAuth.

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description: Callback URL. Must match the redirect URI registered for this client in Anaplan Administration.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Opaque value used to protect against CSRF attacks. The authorization server returns this value unchanged in the redirect.

302.description: Redirect to the Anaplan login page. On successful user authentication, the browser is redirected to `redirect_uri?code={authorization_code}&state={state}`.

post.description:
Minimum role: None.

Starts the Device Authorization flow. The client sends its `client_id` and requested `scope`. The response contains a `device_code` (for polling) and a `user_code` the user must enter at `verification_uri` to approve access.

After receiving this response, the client polls `POST /oauth/token` with `grant_type: urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code` at the `interval` frequency until the user approves or the code expires.

client_id.description: Your device grant OAuth client ID.

scope.description: Space-separated list of requested scopes. Optional - the server applies a default scope if omitted (RFC 8628).

audience.description: Target audience for the issued token. Optional.

200.description: Device authorization started. Display `user_code` and `verification_uri` to the user and poll `POST /oauth/token` until approval or expiry.

post.summary: Exchange Code or Refresh Token for Access Token
post.description:
Minimum role: None.

Issues access tokens, ID tokens, and refresh tokens. Supports three grant types:

- **`authorization_code`**: Exchange the code from `GET /auth/authorize` for tokens. Requires `client_secret`.
- **`urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code`**: Poll during the Device Authorization flow. Returns `authorization_pending` while awaiting user approval; returns tokens once approved. If polling too fast, returns `slow_down`.
- **`refresh_token`**: Obtain a new access token from a valid refresh token. `client_secret` required for Authorization Code clients; omit for Device Grant clients.

Refresh tokens may be rotatable (new refresh token issued on each use, 30-day rotation) or non-rotatable, depending on client configuration.

code.description: Authorization code received from the `GET /auth/authorize` redirect callback.

client_secret.description: Your application's client secret, found in Anaplan Administration > OAuth.

redirect_uri.description: Must exactly match the redirect URI used in the `GET /auth/authorize` request.

client_id.description: Your device grant OAuth client ID.

refresh_token.description: Refresh token from a prior `POST /oauth/token` response (`refresh_token`).

client_secret.description: Client secret. Required for Authorization Code Grant clients; omit for Device Grant clients.
